I understand these tests are not good, but can you please explain how adding assertions makes them better?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

I was going through code smells on sonarcloud and in the blockers section these test cases are showing as a code smells because every test case need at least one assertion statement. So i was trying to lower the code smells.

> > > I was going through code smells on sonarcloud and in the blockers section these test cases are showing as a code smells because every test case need at least one assertion statement. So i was trying to lower the code smells.
> > 
> > Sonar is correct here; fix might be to remove the test, not to mask-out the alert.
> 
> So do you have any suggestions for me like how can I contribute ?

I suggest making the tests valid. For AdminAuthExtension I don't have a plain suggestion.

Still, both ArtifcatTest and SecureServicesTest can be rewritten:

A) You may try adding the package `org.onap.vid.asdc.beans` to `org.onap.vid.model.PojoBeanTest`, to see if it works and covers well. (the the relevant tests for the POJOs under org.onap.vid.asdc.beans should be removed)

B) In case A does not work well, you can rewrite each of ArtifcatTest and SecureServicesTest to use relevant asserting from com.google.code.beanmatchers.BeanMatchers. (You may take inspiration from CategoryParameterOptionTest.)


Option A is better as it'll automate most of the needed work. I suggest looking into PojoBeanTest, understand what it does and utilize if to full extension.


What do you think?
